highlights.text: I wrote on Monday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
Two of those building blocks are what I call “elevator down” and “short squeeze” and these are always paired together back-to-back.
Elevator down refers to how when ES sells, does not just grind lower.
When it sells, it is always vertical, and “elevator down”.
My core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
Often, to backtest the region that caused the “elevator down” move.
Therefore, a common cycle goes a little like this: ES goes elevator down, it puts in a failed breakdown, it short squeezes, it backtests.
On Friday, NFP hit and ES lost a major shelf at 6518 and 6505, going “elevator down” selling to 6452 low of day.

I wrote on on Thursday at 4pm that “Bulls want to see us defend the supports mentioned above on any dips (no lower than 6461 or trap below) .” On Friday at 11am we trapped under 6461, Failed Breakdown, rallied to 6490 to close last week.
We sold this morning rapidly (elevator down), lost 6495 by a few points, Failed Breakdown, then squeezed to 6518, then pushed into the close.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll discuss the Failed Breakdown on Friday that gave us the Monday squeeze (this is important to know), then I’ll talk the actionable trade plan for tomorrow.
6495 to 6518= a chop range with 6505 magnet.

post_title: Data Catalysts Incoming. Can SPX Make Another All Time High? Sept 10th Plan
